decisions.reasoning: We start with no settlements or resources. All options are valid placements. To maximize future production and port access, we should aim for a node with strong resource yields and a port. Node 25, 26, 38, 39, 48, 49, 52, 53 have 3:1 ports, which are highly valuable for trading. Among these, node 25 and 26 offer BRICK 3:1 port and have BRICK production (on 4 and 5), which is common and useful. Node 25 and 26 also have BRICK on 5 (4 pips), which is a high-odds roll (4 pips = 4 ways to roll). Choosing a port-rich node early gives flexibility in trading and resource acquisition. Option [25] and [26] are both strong, but [25] is listed first and offers BRICK on 5 (4 pips) with a 3:1 port — a solid balance of yield and access. Thus, we pick [25].

Choosing (24,25) is symmetric and safe; (25,26) is also valid but less central. Either is acceptable, but (24,25) is slightly more strategic as 24 is adjacent to more tiles. Pick first valid option.
